name: AI Technology Ontology
description: A structured knowledge base organizing the AI technology stack into 6 layers and 9 perspectives, covering from physical accelerators to end-user applications.

hardware.subtitle: AI Accelerators
hardware.description: Physical compute accelerators — GPUs, TPUs, and specialized AI chips — that provide the raw computational substrate for model training and inference.

items.name: NVIDIA H100
items.summary: NVIDIA's flagship Hopper-architecture GPU, the dominant accelerator for large-scale LLM training and high-throughput inference.

cost.note: ~$30k-40k per unit; cloud rental ~$2-4/hr

items.name: NVIDIA H200
items.summary: NVIDIA's next-generation Hopper GPU with HBM3e memory, optimized for inference of large models with improved memory bandwidth.

cost.note: ~$40k+ per unit; cloud rental ~$3-5/hr

items.name: NVIDIA A100
items.summary: NVIDIA's Ampere-architecture GPU, widely deployed in cloud and on-premise AI infrastructure, still the most common training accelerator in production.

cost.note: ~$10k-15k per unit; cloud rental ~$1-2/hr

items.name: Google TPU v5
items.summary: Google's fifth-generation Tensor Processing Unit, purpose-built for large-scale ML training and inference within Google Cloud.

cost.note: Google Cloud only; competitive with H100 for specific workloads

items.name: Groq LPU
items.summary: Groq's Language Processing Unit, a deterministic inference chip delivering industry-leading token generation speed for LLM serving.

cost.note: Currently free tier available; very competitive inference pricing

items.name: Apple Silicon (M-series)
items.summary: Apple's unified memory architecture chips (M1/M2/M3/M4), the leading platform for on-device and local AI inference with exceptional performance-per-watt.

cost.note: Bundled with Mac hardware; no separate GPU cost

items.name: Qualcomm Snapdragon 8 Elite
items.summary: Flagship mobile SoC with dedicated Hexagon NPU for on-device AI inference and multimodal processing.

cost.note: Bundled with mobile devices; ~$200-300 per device

items.name: MediaTek Dimensity 9400
items.summary: High-performance mobile chipset with AI processing unit optimized for edge AI workloads.

cost.note: Bundled with mobile devices; competitive pricing vs Snapdragon

items.name: Google Tensor G4
items.summary: Google's custom mobile chip optimized for on-device AI and Gemini Nano integration.

cost.note: Bundled with Pixel devices; Google-exclusive

items.name: NVIDIA Jetson Orin
items.summary: Edge AI platform for robotics, autonomous machines and embedded systems with up to 275 TOPS performance.

cost.note: ~$500-2000 per module depending on variant

items.name: Hailo-8
items.summary: Edge AI processor delivering up to 26 TOPS for high-performance inference in embedded devices.

cost.note: ~$100-300 per chip; cost-effective for edge deployment

items.name: Intel Movidius
items.summary: Vision processing unit (VPU) for edge AI applications with focus on computer vision workloads.

cost.note: ~$50-200 per unit; very cost-effective for vision tasks

items.name: NVLink
items.summary: NVIDIA's high-bandwidth GPU-to-GPU interconnect enabling multi-GPU training with up to 900GB/s bandwidth.

cost.note: Bundled with H100/A100 GPUs; critical for multi-GPU training

items.name: NVSwitch
items.summary: NVIDIA's switch fabric enabling all-to-all GPU communication in DGX systems with full NVLink bandwidth.

cost.note: Bundled with DGX systems; enables 8+ GPU configurations

infra.subtitle: Infrastructure
infra.description: The compute, storage and serving layer that makes AI systems run at scale.

items.name: CUDA
items.summary: NVIDIA's parallel computing platform, the de facto standard for AI model training and inference.

cost.note: Free SDK; cost is NVIDIA GPU hardware

items.name: ROCm
items.summary: AMD's open-source GPU compute platform, emerging alternative to CUDA for AI workloads.

cost.note: Open-source; cost is AMD GPU hardware (cheaper than NVIDIA)

items.name: vLLM
items.summary: High-throughput LLM inference engine with PagedAttention, the dominant open-source serving solution.

cost.note: Open-source; cost is GPU infrastructure only

items.name: TGI
items.summary: HuggingFace's production-grade LLM serving toolkit with built-in quantization and streaming support.

cost.note: Open-source; HuggingFace Inference Endpoints available as managed option

items.name: Ollama
items.summary: Dead-simple local LLM runner that democratized self-hosted model deployment for developers.

cost.note: Completely free; runs on consumer hardware

items.name: PyTorch
items.summary: The dominant deep learning framework powering most AI research and production training pipelines.

cost.note: Open-source; cost is compute infrastructure

items.name: TensorFlow
items.summary: Google's deep learning framework, largely displaced by PyTorch in research but still active in production.

cost.note: Open-source; cost is compute infrastructure

items.name: Unsloth
items.summary: Fine-tuning accelerator that reduces VRAM usage and speeds up LoRA training by 2-5x.

cost.note: Open-source community version free; Pro version for larger models

items.name: Milvus
items.summary: Cloud-native vector database built for billion-scale similarity search in production RAG pipelines.

cost.note: Open-source self-hosted free; Zilliz Cloud managed service available

items.name: Qdrant
items.summary: High-performance vector search engine with payload filtering.

cost.note: Open-source self-hosted free; Qdrant Cloud managed service available

items.name: Weaviate
items.summary: Vector database with built-in vectorization and hybrid search combining vector and keyword search.

cost.note: Open-source self-hosted free; Weaviate Cloud managed service available

items.name: Chroma
items.summary: Lightweight embedding database designed for AI applications with minimal setup.

cost.note: Fully open-source; very lightweight for prototyping

items.name: Pinecone
items.summary: Managed vector database for production RAG systems with serverless scaling.

cost.note: Free tier available; paid plans from $70/month; serverless pricing

items.name: Mem0
items.summary: Persistent memory layer for AI agents enabling cross-session user preference and context retention.

cost.note: Open-source self-hosted free; cloud managed plan available

items.name: Zep
items.summary: Long-term memory store for LLM apps with built-in temporal reasoning over conversation history.

cost.note: Open-source community edition; Zep Cloud for managed service

items.name: LiteLLM
items.summary: Unified LLM proxy that normalizes 100+ model APIs into a single OpenAI-compatible interface.

cost.note: Open-source self-hosted free; LiteLLM Enterprise for advanced features

security.note: Acts as proxy; all traffic passes through; audit logging available

items.name: Ray
items.summary: Distributed computing framework for AI workloads with unified API for training, serving and data processing.

cost.note: Open-source free; Anyscale managed platform available

runtime.subtitle: Models & Protocols
runtime.description: Core runtime layer: frontier models that provide intelligence, protocols that standardize tool and agent communication, and memory systems that enable context persistence across sessions.

items.name: GPT-4o
items.summary: OpenAI's flagship multimodal model, de facto standard for API-based LLM integration.

cost.note: Input ~$2.5/1M tokens, Output ~$10/1M tokens (gpt-4o)

items.name: o3
items.summary: OpenAI's reasoning-specialized model using extended thinking at inference time for complex problem solving.

cost.note: Input ~$10/1M tokens, Output ~$40/1M tokens; thinking tokens add cost

items.name: Claude 3.7 Sonnet
items.summary: Anthropic's leading model with extended thinking mode, strongest in agentic coding and long-context tasks.

cost.note: Input ~$3/1M tokens, Output ~$15/1M tokens

items.name: Gemini 2.5 Pro
items.summary: Google's frontier model with 1M context window, leading in long-document and multimodal understanding.

cost.note: Input ~$1.25/1M tokens (≤200k), Output ~$10/1M tokens

items.name: Llama 3
items.summary: Meta's open-weight flagship, the dominant base model for self-hosted and fine-tuned deployments.

cost.note: Free weights; cost is compute infrastructure only

items.name: Mistral Large
items.summary: Mistral's top-tier model balancing strong performance with efficient inference.

cost.note: Input ~$2/1M tokens, Output ~$6/1M tokens; competitive pricing

items.name: DeepSeek R1
items.summary: Chinese open-weight reasoning model matching frontier performance at a fraction of training cost.

cost.note: Free weights; API via DeepSeek ~$0.14/1M input tokens

security.note: Chinese jurisdiction; data sovereignty concerns for enterprise use

items.name: MCP
items.summary: Anthropic's open protocol standardizing how AI models connect to external tools and data sources.

cost.note: Open protocol; no licensing cost; implementation effort required

security.note: Tool servers can access sensitive data; requires careful permission design

items.name: A2A
items.summary: Google's Agent-to-Agent protocol enabling standardized communication between autonomous AI agents.

cost.note: Open protocol; early stage, ecosystem still forming

items.name: RT-2 (Robotic Transformer)
items.summary: Vision-language-action model bridging language understanding and robotic control for embodied AI.

cost.note: Research model; not publicly available for production use

items.name: OpenVLA
items.summary: Open-source vision-language-action model for robotic manipulation trained on diverse datasets.

cost.note: Open weights; cost is compute infrastructure for inference

items.name: Octo
items.summary: Generalist robot policy trained on diverse manipulation datasets for cross-embodiment transfer.

cost.note: Open weights; research-focused model

reasoning.subtitle: Reasoning Paradigms
reasoning.description: The cognitive logic layer that defines how to transform raw intelligence from models into problem-solving capabilities. These paradigms represent fundamental thinking patterns and constraints that bridge the gap between model capabilities and real-world applications.

items.name: RAG
items.summary: Retrieval-Augmented Generation grounds LLM outputs in external knowledge, dominant pattern for reducing hallucination.

cost.note: Cost depends on vector DB + embedding model; adds latency overhead

items.name: Chain-of-Thought
items.summary: Prompting technique eliciting step-by-step reasoning, foundational to modern agent planning and reasoning models.

cost.note: Increases token usage 2-5x; higher cost but better accuracy

items.name: Prompt Engineering
items.summary: Systematic design of model inputs to reliably elicit desired outputs.

cost.note: No direct cost; requires engineering time investment

items.name: Function Calling
items.summary: Structured mechanism for LLMs to invoke external tools via JSON schema, foundation of all tool-use agents.

cost.note: Minimal token overhead for schema definition; tool execution cost varies

security.note: Tool execution can have side effects; requires careful permission scoping

items.name: Fine-tuning
items.summary: Adapting a pretrained model on domain-specific data to improve task performance.

cost.note: GPU hours required; LoRA reduces cost significantly vs full fine-tuning

items.name: Context Window
items.summary: The finite token budget available to a model per inference call, the primary constraint shaping all reasoning paradigm decisions including RAG, memory architecture, and prompt engineering strategies.

cost.note: Larger context = higher cost; 1M token context can cost $1-10 per call

security.note: All data in context window is sent to model provider

orchestration.subtitle: Workflow & Automation
orchestration.description: Connecting models, tools and memory into executable task flows that automate real-world processes.

items.name: Temporal
items.summary: Durable workflow engine that persists execution state, enabling long-running and fault-tolerant AI task pipelines.

cost.note: Open-source self-hosted free; Temporal Cloud managed service ~$0.00025/workflow action

items.name: Prefect
items.summary: Python-native workflow orchestration with dynamic DAG support, increasingly adopted for AI pipeline management.

cost.note: Open-source free; Prefect Cloud free tier available; Pro plan for teams

items.name: Airflow
items.summary: Battle-tested DAG-based workflow scheduler, widely used in data pipelines but increasingly stretched by AI workloads.

cost.note: Apache open-source; cost is infrastructure only

items.name: Kubeflow
items.summary: Kubernetes-native ML workflow orchestration platform for training, serving and pipeline management.

cost.note: Open-source; cost is Kubernetes infrastructure

items.name: LangGraph
items.summary: Stateful graph-based orchestration for multi-agent systems, emerging standard for complex agent workflows.

cost.note: Open-source library; LangGraph Cloud for managed deployment

items.name: CrewAI
items.summary: Role-based multi-agent framework focused on collaborative task execution with minimal boilerplate.

cost.note: Open-source free; CrewAI Enterprise for production support

items.name: Dify
items.summary: Visual LLM workflow builder with built-in RAG, tool integration and one-click deployment for production agents.

cost.note: Open-source self-hosted free; Dify Cloud free tier + paid plans

items.name: Flowise
items.summary: Open-source drag-and-drop UI for building LangChain-based agent workflows without writing code.

cost.note: Fully open-source; self-hosted at no cost

items.name: n8n
items.summary: Open-source workflow automation platform rapidly adopted as the self-hosted backbone for AI-augmented business processes.

cost.note: Self-hosted free (fair-code license); n8n Cloud from $20/month

items.name: Zapier
items.summary: Cloud automation platform connecting 6000+ apps, adding AI actions as a layer on top of existing integrations.

cost.note: Free tier (100 tasks/month); paid from $19.99/month; scales with task volume

security.note: All data flows through Zapier servers; review data handling for sensitive workflows

items.name: Browser Use
items.summary: Open-source library enabling LLMs to control browsers via structured actions, foundation of web automation agents.

cost.note: Open-source library; cost is LLM API calls for element targeting

security.note: Browser agent can access any page content; sandbox execution recommended

items.name: Stagehand
items.summary: Browserbase's AI web automation SDK combining Playwright with LLM-powered element targeting.

cost.note: Open-source SDK free; Browserbase cloud for managed browser sessions

items.name: Playwright
items.summary: Microsoft's cross-browser automation library, the dominant low-level substrate for AI browser control agents.

cost.note: Fully open-source; no cost

items.name: LangSmith
items.summary: Tracing and evaluation platform for LLM applications, tightly integrated with the LangChain ecosystem.

cost.note: Free tier (5k traces/month); Developer $39/month; Plus $299/month

security.note: Trace data including prompts/responses sent to LangSmith servers

items.name: Langfuse
items.summary: Open-source LLM observability platform with tracing, evals and prompt management for production pipelines.

cost.note: Open-source self-hosted free; Langfuse Cloud free tier + paid plans

items.name: Agentic Loop
items.summary: The plan-act-observe cycle at the core of autonomous agents, enabling iterative task completion without human intervention.

cost.note: Each loop iteration consumes LLM tokens; uncontrolled loops can be expensive

security.note: Autonomous execution without oversight; requires careful tool permission scoping

items.name: Human-in-the-Loop
items.summary: Design pattern inserting human approval or correction checkpoints into agent workflows to ensure safety and accuracy.

cost.note: Adds human review time; reduces automation ROI but critical for high-risk tasks

security.note: Human oversight significantly reduces autonomous agent risk

application.subtitle: Products & Platforms
application.description: End-user products and platforms where AI capability is delivered as tangible value.

items.name: ChatGPT
items.summary: The product that brought LLMs to mainstream consumers, still the dominant AI assistant by user volume.

cost.note: Free tier available; ChatGPT Plus $20/month; Team $25/user/month; Enterprise custom

security.note: Conversations may be used for training unless opted out; Enterprise has stronger data controls

items.name: Claude
items.summary: Anthropic's consumer assistant, leading in long-form reasoning, document analysis and coding tasks.

cost.note: Free tier available; Claude Pro $20/month; Team $25/user/month

security.note: Strong Constitutional AI safety focus; Enterprise tier with data isolation

items.name: Gemini
items.summary: Google's consumer AI assistant deeply integrated with Workspace, strongest in multimodal and real-time search.

cost.note: Free tier available; Gemini Advanced $19.99/month (Google One AI Premium)

security.note: Google Workspace integration; Workspace data not used for training by default

items.name: Cursor
items.summary: AI-native code editor that redefined developer workflow, the fastest-growing dev tool in AI era.

cost.note: Free tier (2000 completions); Pro $20/month; Business $40/user/month

security.note: Code sent to Cursor servers for AI processing; Privacy Mode available to disable storage

items.name: Antigravity
items.summary: Google's agent-first IDE with multi-agent orchestration, browser/terminal control, and strong enterprise capabilities.

cost.note: Free tier available; paid plans for advanced agent features

security.note: Google enterprise-grade security; strong data isolation for enterprise customers

items.name: GitHub Copilot
items.summary: Mature mainstream coding platform with deep IDE integration, enterprise security, and evolving agentic features.

cost.note: Free for verified students/OSS; Individual $10/month; Business $19/user/month; Enterprise $39/user/month

security.note: Enterprise: code not used for training; private model option; strong compliance posture

items.name: Midjourney
items.summary: Leading AI image generation platform known for aesthetic quality.

cost.note: Basic $10/month (200 images); Standard $30/month; Pro $60/month; Mega $120/month

security.note: All generated images public by default on lower tiers; Stealth mode on Pro+

items.name: Stable Diffusion
items.summary: Open-weight image generation model democratizing visual AI.

cost.note: Open weights free to download; cost is GPU compute for inference

security.note: Self-hosted; full data control; no external data transmission

items.name: ElevenLabs
items.summary: Ultra-realistic AI voice synthesis and cloning platform.

cost.note: Free tier (10k chars/month); Starter $5/month; Creator $22/month; Pro $99/month

security.note: Voice cloning raises consent/misuse concerns; platform has abuse detection

items.name: Perplexity
items.summary: AI-native search engine delivering cited answers in real time.

cost.note: Free tier available; Perplexity Pro $20/month for unlimited Pro searches

security.note: Search queries processed on Perplexity servers; no persistent user data by default

items.name: Rabbit R1
items.summary: Standalone AI companion device with LAM-powered app control and natural language interface.

cost.note: $199 device; no subscription required

security.note: Processes data in cloud; app credentials stored on device

items.name: Humane AI Pin
items.summary: Wearable AI assistant with projector interface, camera and multimodal input for screenless interaction.

cost.note: $699 device + $24/month subscription

security.note: All processing in cloud; always-on camera raises privacy concerns

items.name: Meta Ray-Ban Smart Glasses
items.summary: AR glasses with integrated multimodal AI assistant, camera and real-time visual understanding.

cost.note: $299-379 depending on model; no subscription

security.note: Camera and audio processed by Meta; privacy LED indicator
